title: RTX 4060 Laptop 8GB + Ryzen 8845H/8655H local NPU profile
scope: Implemented the 4060 8GB laptop profile as local DNA LLM on AMD XDNA/NPU budget plus local VoxCPM2 int8 TTS and Ditto TensorRT avatar on GPU.
Aligned naia-shell settings manifest generation with naia-omni-windows-manager loader planning.
Fixed manifest-based NVA directory resolution for shell-launched output cascade.
Rechecked Windows distribution build impact after the loader/profile changes.

notes: Sequential verification was used after confirming no active git index locks in the relevant repos.
Other live Codex/opencode processes existed, but this work was completed under a local coordination lock.

verification.command: python -m pytest tests/test_profiles_vram_fit.py tests/test_launcher.py tests/test_manifest.py tests/test_service_plan.py
pnpm --dir packages/shell exec playwright test e2e/capability-settings.spec.ts e2e/nva-p0-sync-capability.spec.ts

verification.result: health ok, TTS true, avatar true, output D:\alpha-adk\tmp\naia-cascade-smoke-coord-20260720-031108.webm, duration 4.308s, size 1428123 bytes
NSIS and MSI build completed; only WiX ICE warnings remained

review_notes: The profile no longer treats the 4060 8GB laptop path as cloud LLM/TTS. LLM is local by design, budgeted outside GPU VRAM as the AMD NPU route.
The Windows manager still does not install or benchmark the AMD NPU runtime itself; it preserves the GPU placement and service plan around that local NPU assumption.
naia-adk local settings and terminology changes were intentionally left out because they appear to be user-local workspace state.
No new verify-* skill was created because existing build, deployment, Playwright, and loader unit tests cover the changed behavior.
